codeReview.summary: The fix directly addresses the prior blocking defect: retry_request() no longer retries broad RequestException subclasses and is now constrained to transient HTTP statuses plus connection-oriented failures (ConnectionError, Timeout). The added negative tests prove non-transient RequestException subclasses fail fast, and added recovery tests confirm transient failures still retry and return valid responses.

issues.description: backoff_delays length validation still requires len(backoff_delays) >= max_retries even though only max_retries-1 delays are consumed between attempts. This is a pre-existing off-by-one validation mismatch with the docstring and can reject otherwise valid custom delay tuples.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Test-result evidence can be masked when test commands are piped (e.g., to tail), producing a successful shell exit code even when tests fail; current mission guidance does not explicitly warn against this.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Fix transcript skeleton includes `python3 -m unittest discover -s tests -p 'test_*.py' -v 2>&1 | tail -40`, then notes a failing flaky test, while handoff records `exitCode: 0` for that command.

summary: Reviewed both diffs (original failure commit abe6e95 and fix commit 9193696), the prior scrutiny review, and the fix worker transcript skeleton. The fix adequately resolves the original blocking retry-scope regression and adds contract-aligned negative coverage for non-transient RequestException subclasses, so this review passes.
